mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts toward recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real retail sales and food services revenue signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions trend upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates (Spearman rho < 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production below trend signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when INDPRO contracts, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts and real retail sales signals synchronized demand collapse across residential and consumption channels, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession probability spikes and equity risk premium widens.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (measured as deviation from additive single-series effects) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate combined with elevated federal funds rate signals a policy-induced labor market slowdown that reduces earnings growth expectations and increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation measured by CPI above 3.0% combined with elevated federal funds rate signals persistent monetary tightness that constrains equity valuations through higher real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with restrictive policy response
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of inflation and policy rates does not significantly predict forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly at levels below 2.0% annualized signals demand-side weakness that constrains earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations reset downward and equity risk premium expands.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22768.866 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with low GDP growth periods (Spearman rho > -0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.
